name: traceway/opentelemetry-symfony
description: Pure-PHP OpenTelemetry instrumentation for Symfony — automatic HTTP, Console, HttpClient, Messenger, Doctrine DBAL, Cache, Twig tracing and Monolog log-trace correlation with response propagation, a lightweight Tracing helper, route templates, and semantic conventions. No C extension required (ext-protobuf recommended for production).

suggest.open-telemetry/exporter-otlp: Required to export traces via OTLP (the most common protocol for OpenTelemetry backends)
suggest.php-http/guzzle7-adapter: HTTP transport for the OTLP exporter (or use any PSR-18 client)
suggest.ext-protobuf: Significantly faster protobuf serialization for OTLP export (use with OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_PROTOCOL=http/protobuf)
suggest.doctrine/dbal: Required for automatic Doctrine DBAL query tracing (^4.0)
suggest.symfony/cache: Required for automatic cache pool tracing (get/delete/invalidateTags)
suggest.symfony/http-client: Required for automatic HttpClient outgoing request tracing
suggest.symfony/messenger: Required for automatic Messenger job/task tracing
suggest.twig/twig: Required for automatic Twig template rendering tracing
suggest.monolog/monolog: Required for automatic log-trace correlation (injects trace_id/span_id into log records)
